stories.story: The FBI charged Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, on Thursday with trying to travel overseas to fight for ISIS. Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, is one of three women arrested this week on terror charges. Two New York women were also taken into custody.
key_facts: FBI charged Philadelphia woman Keonna Thomas on Thursday for trying to travel to fight for ISIS
Keonna Thomas is one of three women arrested this week on terror charges
Two New York women were also taken into custody

stories.story: An FBI complaint cites social media messages dating back to August 2013 sent by Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman. Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, wrote that if people knew the realities, they would rush to join brothers in the front lines and pray ALLAH accept them as shuhada [martyrs]. Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, also wrote that when you're a mujahid [violent jihadi fighter] your death becomes a wedding. The FBI said Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, purchased an electronic visa to Turkey on March 23. Turkey is known as the easiest place from which to enter Syria and join ISIS.
key_facts: Keonna Thomas sent social media messages since August 2013
Keonna Thomas wrote about joining brothers in front lines as shuhada and mujahid deaths
Keonna Thomas purchased an electronic visa to Turkey on March 23
Turkey is the easiest place to enter Syria and join ISIS

stories.story: An ISIS manual advises recruits to buy round-trip tickets to vacation spots such as Spain and then purchase tickets for their real destination once they arrive overseas, the FBI said. On March 26, Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, purchased a ticket to Barcelona, with a March 29 departure and an April 15 return to the United States. It's not clear when or where Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, was arrested.
key_facts: ISIS manual suggests buying tickets to vacation spots like Spain first
Keonna Thomas purchased a Barcelona ticket on March 26 with a March 29 departure and April 15 return
explicit_unknowns: when or where Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, was arrested

stories.story: Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, was charged with knowingly attempting to provide material support and resources to a designated foreign terrorist organization. Keonna Thomas, 30, also known as "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," a Philadelphia woman, could be sentenced to 15 years in prison. On Thursday, Noelle Velentzas, 28, and her former roommate, Asia Siddiqui, 31, were arrested in New York and accused of planning to build an explosive device for attacks in the United States.
key_facts: Keonna Thomas charged with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization
Keonna Thomas faces up to 15 years in prison
Noelle Velentzas, 28, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, arrested Thursday in New York for planning an explosive device

stories.story: Noelle Velentzas, 28, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, were arrested in New York and accused of planning to build an explosive device for attacks in the United States. In the past 18 months, the Justice Department's National Security Division has prosecuted or is prosecuting more than 30 cases of people attempting to travel abroad to join or provide support to terrorist groups. Of those cases, 18 allegedly involve support to ISIS. Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson told reporters Thursday that the terrorist threat is more decentralized, more diffuse, and more complicated. Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson said it involves the potential lone wolf actor and the effective use of social media and the Internet.
key_facts: Noelle Velentzas, 28, and Asia Siddiqui, 31, arrested in New York for planning an explosive device
Justice Department's National Security Division has 30+ travel cases in 18 months
18 of those cases allegedly involve support to ISIS
Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson described the threat as decentralized, diffuse, and complicated
Homeland Security Secretary Jeh Johnson noted the role of lone wolf actors and social media/Internet
